title: A Usable and Robust Continuous Authentication Framework Using Wearables

method.input_representation: 84-dimensional feature vector (72 time-domain and 12 frequency-domain features extracted from 6-axis motion sensor data)
method.training_strategy: leave-one-out cross-validation for authentication; 5-fold cross-validation for insider threat identification

evaluation.context: Achieved with sample size of 2,750 using Manhattan (Cityblock) distance on Typing Task-1 dataset

evaluation_protocol: Leave-one-out cross-validation for authentication tasks; separate training and testing tasks for insider threat identification

reported_limitations: Single wrist data collection may miss certain keystroke patterns
Trade-off between data collection time and error rate (EER)

energy_consumption.unit: percent per 10 minutes (LG G Watch R @ 100Hz)

usability.usability_issues: Requires an extra communication channel between the wearable and the terminal

key_contribution: Introduced WACA, a wearable-assisted continuous authentication framework using sensor-based keystroke dynamics
Demonstrated robustness against imitation and statistical bot attacks
Proposed a method for high-accuracy insider threat identification using MLP
main_strength: Strong resistance to sophisticated mimicry attacks
High accuracy for identifying insider attackers (up to 99.2%)
main_weakness: Lack of testing in completely uncontrolled real-world environments
Dependence on the user wearing a smartwatch on the typing hand
insights.notes_for_review: The study highlights a significant trade-off between the time needed to collect biometric data (e.g., 20s vs 30s) and the resulting EER.
